flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of Binary Search on sorted data?
flashcards.back: O(log n). Binary search requires the input data to be sorted and operates in logarithmic time.
Counting Sort and Radix Sort. These algorithms sort data without comparing elements directly.

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of an in-place sorting algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(1). In-place sorting algorithms use constant additional space, modifying the input array directly.

flashcards.front: Why is stability important in sorting?
flashcards.back: Stability ensures that equal elements retain their relative order, which is crucial for sorting complex data like database records.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between adaptive and non-adaptive sorting algorithms?
flashcards.back: Adaptive algorithms perform faster on nearly sorted data (e.g., Insertion Sort), while non-adaptive algorithms (e.g., Merge Sort) have consistent performance regardless of input order.
